summary.one_liner: Analyzed 89 written reviews from the App Store spread across 5 Countries.

quotes.author: laughingull
quotes.text: It makes it easy to search through your personal collection of physical and digital cookbooks looking for recipes, ideas about using ingredients and the like.
quotes.full_text:
A treasure for the cook with too many cookbooks

This app fills a unique niche: it’s for the cook who owns too many cookbooks. It makes it easy to search through your personal collection of physical and digital cookbooks looking for recipes, ideas about using ingredients and the like. If you just have a handful of cookbooks, you don’t need it. But if you’ve bought a number of cookbooks over the years and you’ve added them to the Eat your Books site, this app makes it really easy to search.
Is it perfect? No. But I don’t know of anything else like it. And it’s so useful. 
Yes, the app costs money. Programming takes time. Updating programs like this one takes time. But think of it this way: For a relatively small cost, you can restore the usefulness of those cookbooks that you bought over the years. You’ll save yourself time. Rediscoveries await.
quotes.excerpt: It makes it easy to search through your personal collection of physical and digital cookbooks looking for recipes, ideas about using ingredients and the like.

quotes.author: jv4
quotes.text: I love being able to quickly find recipes, but even better is quickly searching by ingredient. For example, right now I have an open jar of chilies in adobo sauce to use up. I was able to instantly find quite a few recipes from my collection that use that ingredient.
quotes.full_text:
Almost Perfect

I have the free version with my top 5 cookbooks loaded. I love being able to quickly find recipes, but even better is quickly searching by ingredient. For example, right now I have an open jar of chilies in adobo sauce to use up. I was able to instantly find quite a few recipes from my collection that use that ingredient. Perfect if you’re at home and trying to plan meals. This isn’t 5 stars for me yet because it doesn’t list quantities. So if I’m looking up a recipe while at the store or away from my cookbooks, I had to guess how much to buy. I’m sure there is licensing reasons for this, but it’s still something to be aware of. Also, I’ll add more cookbooks if the Pro version pricing comes down. Right now it’s too expensive to justify.
quotes.excerpt: I love being able to quickly find recipes, but even better is quickly searching by ingredient. For example, right now I have an open jar of chilies in adobo sauce to use up. I…

quotes.author: iharrex
quotes.text: I've collected thousands of dollars worth of cookbooks and have spent years looking for recipes online instead, so that I don't have to go through every book.
quotes.full_text:
Love love love this app

Finally an app that translates to less time on phone, more time in real life!
This was advertised to me a few weeks back. I did a trial, fell in love, and am using it daily. Happy to pay for something that makes using my cookbook trove actually enjoyable, not a chore! I’ve collected thousands of dollars worth of cookbooks and have spent years looking for recipes online instead, so that I don’t have to go through every book. 
Thank you thank you thank you for creating such a useful app! Am recommending it to everyone!
quotes.excerpt: I've collected thousands of dollars worth of cookbooks and have spent years looking for recipes online instead, so that I don't have to go through every book.

quotes.author: AlabamaSweetHome
quotes.text: I also love that you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store, just in case you forgot something or want to pursue a new idea.
quotes.full_text:
I didn’t expect to like this, but it’s great!

From my brief use of the Cookshelf app, I can tell that it will be really helpful to me.  I’m not great at Meal Planning, but I think I can manage it better using Cookshelf.  I also love that you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store, just in case you forgot something or want to pursue a new idea. It’s a great companion to Eat Your Books, which I love,love,love. I think it may take a few days to get Cookshelf figured out, but I look forward to exploring what Cookshelf can do.
quotes.excerpt: I also love that you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store, just in case you forgot something or want to pursue a new idea.

quotes.author: osucristina
quotes.text: It's like searching a recipe app but if your own cookbooks. Sure, you could flip though a bunch of books and find a recipe you want to make but why do that when you can search the app for "ground turkey" and it will pull up every recipe from your cookbooks that use that ingredient.
quotes.full_text:
Game Changer!

Ok, I got a 30 day trial (shoutout Anchovy Book Co!) and thought it would be useless (hello I already have all the cookbooks in print, what do I need an app?) and I was SO WRONG. This is amazing. It’s like searching a recipe app but if your own cookbooks. Sure, you could flip though a bunch of books and find a recipe you want to make but why do that when you can search the app for “ground turkey” and it will pull up every recipe from your cookbooks that use that ingredient. Love the comments feature too!
quotes.excerpt: It's like searching a recipe app but if your own cookbooks. Sure, you could flip though a bunch of books and find a recipe you want to make but why do that when you can search…

quotes.author: WyndleCook
quotes.text: Have you've ever bough a specific ingredient for a single recipe and now wonder what the heck to do with the rest of it? This app if for you! I love that I can search across all of my books for a single ingredient and see reviews of recipes from other cooks.
quotes.full_text:
Love your cookbooks again (or even more!)

Have you’ve ever bough a specific ingredient for a single recipe and now wonder what the heck to do with the rest of it? This app if for you! I no longer feel guilty about buying pomegranate molasses, kaffir lime leaves, or harissa paste.  

I have a major cookbook problem and CookShelf has made my addiction manageable. I love that I can search across all of my books for a single ingredient and see reviews of recipes from other cooks. It’s helped me rediscover lost loves and find new ones.
quotes.excerpt: Have you've ever bough a specific ingredient for a single recipe and now wonder what the heck to do with the rest of it? This app if for you! I love that I can search across all…

quotes.author: Wobbly2025
quotes.text: I couldn't find an ingredient for a salad I had planned. But with CookShelf on my phone, I was able to look up recipes using what was available.
quotes.full_text:
Saved me a another shopping trip

I like to plan out my meals for the week and then shop for what I need for those meals. Today at the grocery store, I couldn't find an ingredient for a salad I had planned. But with CookShelf on my phone, I was able to look up recipes using what was available. The whole recipe wasn’t there, but the ingredients I needed to make it were listed. I didn’t have to go home, find another recipe for that night, and make another shopping trip. Yeah!
quotes.excerpt: I couldn't find an ingredient for a salad I had planned. But with CookShelf on my phone, I was able to look up recipes using what was available.

quotes.author: welikegoodfood
quotes.text: We love how we can search by ingredients. No more flipping through indexes or leafing through cookbooks with no indexes.
quotes.full_text:
We love this app

This apps unlocks the power of our 90 odd cookbooks.  We love how we can search by ingredients.  No more flipping through indexes or leafing through cookbooks with no indexes.  We have started using most of our cookbooks again.  We wish it was offered at a more affordable price point.  We think it is overpriced; however now that we have it we definitely use it multiple times every week.
quotes.excerpt: We love how we can search by ingredients. No more flipping through indexes or leafing through cookbooks with no indexes.

quotes.author: plumberful
quotes.text: Being able to search by ingredients is a game-changer and I also find it very helpful to quickly search for specific recipes from specific books.
quotes.full_text:
Terrific App!

The CookShelf App has made my cookbook collection so much more useable! Being able to search by ingredients is a game-changer and I also find it very helpful to quickly search for specific recipes from specific books. I love being able to save notes, ratings and changes to recipes I’ve cooked, as well—all in the App. I have rediscovered some real gems on my own bookshelves—Thank you!
quotes.excerpt: Being able to search by ingredients is a game-changer and I also find it very helpful to quickly search for specific recipes from specific books.

quotes.author: Duehanfkfkfksjahag
quotes.text: I can search for an ingredient I have and see what recipes I can make.
quotes.full_text:
One of a kind great

I have so many cookbooks and this is just an amazing tool for getting more use out of them! I love seeing what other people are making from the books I have, and reading their comments. I can search for an ingredient I have and see what recipes I can make. I think the cost is very justified when you consider the work that goes into indexing.
quotes.excerpt: I can search for an ingredient I have and see what recipes I can make.

quotes.author: riceball27
quotes.text: Now I search whatever meat I have and try new recipes from books I hadn't opened in awhile or really got to test out.
quotes.full_text:
Love this app

I collect cookbooks. Anytime I have a new hobby I must find the cookbook to go with it. This app has changed up how I do dinner as before I had so many cookbooks I started to favor some over the others. Now I search whatever meat I have and try new recipes from books I hadn't opened in awhile or really got to test out.
quotes.excerpt: Now I search whatever meat I have and try new recipes from books I hadn't opened in awhile or really got to test out.

quotes.author: TabithaYogi
quotes.text: I finally have a way to search across all of them.
quotes.full_text:
Best app I have

I love collecting cookbooks snd recipes, and I finally have a way to search across all of them. It’s like my cookbooks merged with online searching because of this app.  It’s so fun to scroll through everything in one step. I’ve started cooking more since using the app.  Highly recommend.
quotes.excerpt: I finally have a way to search across all of them.

quotes.author: SW_DC_Gal
quotes.text: My favorite feature is the ingredient list that it gives for recipes I am searching.
quotes.full_text:
Brilliant app

CookShelf is fabulous. My favorite feature is the ingredient list that it gives for recipes I am searching. That helps immensely with determining whether I can make something-do I have the ingredients or pantry staples or will it require a trip to the market? Worth the subscription.
quotes.excerpt: My favorite feature is the ingredient list that it gives for recipes I am searching.

quotes.author: Strangertaste
quotes.text: Have a bunch of cannellini beans on hand? No problem. Scroll through every recipe for them in every cookbook you own.
quotes.full_text:
Love It!

This is a game changer! Have a bunch of cannellini beans on hand? No problem. Scroll through every recipe for them in every cookbook you own. Love that the app quickly shows the other ingredients needed, and gives you the page number to reference. Lovely. ❤️
quotes.excerpt: Have a bunch of cannellini beans on hand? No problem. Scroll through every recipe for them in every cookbook you own.

quotes.author: arugh
quotes.text: Search your cookbooks based on title or ingredients.
quotes.full_text:
Meh

Cool concept. Search your cookbooks based on title or ingredients. But still need to get out your cookbook for quantities and method. Personally wouldn’t pay a $60 annual subscription for this service. Probably would pay a smaller one off price though.
quotes.excerpt: Search your cookbooks based on title or ingredients.

quotes.author: Becky...yes that one.
quotes.text: I used to look at the index of one book after another when I needed a particular recipe.
quotes.full_text:
Super useful

My cookbook collection keeps growing. I used to look at the index of one book after another when I needed a particular recipe.  I might need this app once a month, but it’s still worth the money. Sometimes I just browse random ingredients for fun.
quotes.excerpt: I used to look at the index of one book after another when I needed a particular recipe.

quotes.author: TOTCOOKS
quotes.text: whenever you want to make something but can't remember what recipes are where you just list an ingredient you have and search all your cookbooks to find recipes.
quotes.full_text:
Game changer

So easy to add your cookbooks by scanning the bar codes and whenever you want to make something but can’t remember what recipes are where you just list an ingredient you have and search all your cookbooks to find recipes. It’s so handy; I love it!
quotes.excerpt: whenever you want to make something but can't remember what recipes are where you just list an ingredient you have and search all your cookbooks to find recipes.

quotes.author: Lcb072818
quotes.text: It's nice to be able to just look up an ingredient or dish in the app and know which of my cookbooks have the recipes I need.
quotes.full_text:
Game changer for actually using your cookbooks

I love being able to search my own cookbooks for things to make rather than googling! It’s nice to be able to just look up an ingredient or dish in the app and know which of my cookbooks have the recipes I need.
quotes.excerpt: It's nice to be able to just look up an ingredient or dish in the app and know which of my cookbooks have the recipes I need.

quotes.author: LJS0427
quotes.text: I have over 120 cookbooks so this is something I have been needing for years!!!
quotes.full_text:
My dream all!!

I have over 120 cookbooks so this is something I have been needing for years!!! I will use this everyday and it has already changed the way I cook.  If you love being the kitchen, you need this app.
quotes.excerpt: I have over 120 cookbooks so this is something I have been needing for years!!!

quotes.author: Mastonec
quotes.text: I can put in my veggies on hand and instantly find great recipes from all my cookbooks without having to look through each index.
quotes.full_text:
Eat my produce!

This app is a game changer. I can put in my veggies on hand and instantly find great recipes from all my cookbooks without having to look through each index.
quotes.excerpt: I can put in my veggies on hand and instantly find great recipes from all my cookbooks without having to look through each index.

quotes.author: Bad cook 6
quotes.text: Also good to search for ideas to use food in the fridge.
quotes.full_text:
Amazing way to use your books

Such a great way of using all those books on the shelf which you never look at. Also good to search for ideas to use food in the fridge. Highly recommend.
quotes.excerpt: Also good to search for ideas to use food in the fridge.

quotes.author: Rustlethepetals
quotes.text: I can search every recipe in my collection by ingredient!
quotes.full_text:
Essential for cookbook lovers

As an avid cook with a cookbook collection of nearly 400, this app is a complete game changer. I can search every recipe in my collection by ingredient!
quotes.excerpt: I can search every recipe in my collection by ingredient!

quotes.full_text:
Love this app

If you have a bit of a cookery book addiction this is the app for you.  It’s brilliant for finding recipes even from some older, more obscure books and means you get to know and use your books rather than ending up googling second rate recipes.  I’ve been using it all the time since I downloaded it.  It’s
like receiving an exciting new cookery book in the post every day and actually using it.

quotes.author: ConcerningAda
quotes.text: I'm already planning next week's menu using books that I wouldn't normally consider.
quotes.full_text:
So glad I found this app

I've long struggled with a way to effectively search my cookery book collection, so this is a game changer. It’s really easy to use, especially with the barcode scanner - amazed that even my most obscure titles are indexed. I’m already planning next week's menu using books that I wouldn't normally consider. And justification (not that I need it) for the shelf space!

quotes.full_text:
AMAZING!!

Finally!!! My friends and I have always talked about how much we would like to see all our cookbook and magazine recipes in one place, and this app does it! It’s so quick to setup and so intuitive to use. I am so happy to have this in my cooking toolbox now 😊
quotes.excerpt: My friends and I have always talked about how much we would like to see all our cookbook and magazine recipes in one place, and this app does it!

quotes.author: the-frog
quotes.text: I would use my books more
quotes.full_text:
Great idea, massively overpriced

It’s a cool idea, I would use my books more and would absolutely pay a small convenience fee. But an ongoing $60 subscription is way too much for searching content.
quotes.excerpt: I would use my books more

quotes.author: CG233
quotes.text: Especially when I'm at the grocery store & i want to meal plan/meal prep for the night or the week. It is a little disappointing that the ingredients list don't give to the measurements from the book.
quotes.full_text:
Traveling Kitchen

I’m liking this app so far. Especially when I’m at the grocery store & i want to meal plan/meal prep for the night or the week. It is a little disappointing that the ingredients list don’t give to the measurements from the book. But no big deal. It is also handy that it gives you the page number for the recipe so you can go home & pop open the book when ready to start cooking. Now, the only reason for the 4 star rate is because the app had missing ingredients on the list. I made a peanut butter cake from an old Betty Crocker cookbook that my grandma passed down to me, & it was missing items on list. Thankfully it was a recipe I made before discovering this app. Otherwise, a trip back to the store would have been a pain.
quotes.excerpt: Especially when I'm at the grocery store & i want to meal plan/meal prep for the night or the week. It is a little disappointing that the ingredients list don't give to the…

quotes.author: AlabamaSweetHome
quotes.text: I'm not great at Meal Planning, but I think I can manage it better using Cookshelf. I also love that you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store, just in case you forgot something or want to pursue a new idea.
quotes.full_text:
I didn’t expect to like this, but it’s great!

From my brief use of the Cookshelf app, I can tell that it will be really helpful to me.  I’m not great at Meal Planning, but I think I can manage it better using Cookshelf.  I also love that you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store, just in case you forgot something or want to pursue a new idea. It’s a great companion to Eat Your Books, which I love,love,love. I think it may take a few days to get Cookshelf figured out, but I look forward to exploring what Cookshelf can do.
quotes.excerpt: I'm not great at Meal Planning, but I think I can manage it better using Cookshelf. I also love that you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store,…

quotes.author: Wobbly2025
quotes.text: I like to plan out my meals for the week and then shop for what I need for those meals. Today at the grocery store, I couldn't find an ingredient for a salad I had planned. But with CookShelf on my phone, I was able to look up recipes using what was available.
quotes.full_text:
Saved me a another shopping trip

I like to plan out my meals for the week and then shop for what I need for those meals. Today at the grocery store, I couldn't find an ingredient for a salad I had planned. But with CookShelf on my phone, I was able to look up recipes using what was available. The whole recipe wasn’t there, but the ingredients I needed to make it were listed. I didn’t have to go home, find another recipe for that night, and make another shopping trip. Yeah!
quotes.excerpt: I like to plan out my meals for the week and then shop for what I need for those meals. Today at the grocery store, I couldn't find an ingredient for a salad I had planned. But…

quotes.author: SW_DC_Gal
quotes.text: My favorite feature is the ingredient list that it gives for recipes I am searching. That helps immensely with determining whether I can make something-do I have the ingredients or pantry staples or will it require a trip to the market?
quotes.full_text:
Brilliant app

CookShelf is fabulous. My favorite feature is the ingredient list that it gives for recipes I am searching. That helps immensely with determining whether I can make something-do I have the ingredients or pantry staples or will it require a trip to the market? Worth the subscription.
quotes.excerpt: My favorite feature is the ingredient list that it gives for recipes I am searching. That helps immensely with determining whether I can make something-do I have the ingredients…

quotes.author: GigiAnon
quotes.text: So easy to add my cookbooks and great when I'm at the store and need to check the ingredients I need for a recipe.
quotes.full_text:
Great app!

This app is a joy to use! So easy to add my cookbooks and great when I’m at the store and need to check the ingredients I need for a recipe.
quotes.excerpt: So easy to add my cookbooks and great when I'm at the store and need to check the ingredients I need for a recipe.

quotes.author: ConcerningAda
quotes.text: It's really easy to use, especially with the barcode scanner - amazed that even my most obscure titles are indexed.
quotes.full_text:
So glad I found this app

I've long struggled with a way to effectively search my cookery book collection, so this is a game changer. It’s really easy to use, especially with the barcode scanner - amazed that even my most obscure titles are indexed. I’m already planning next week's menu using books that I wouldn't normally consider. And justification (not that I need it) for the shelf space!
quotes.excerpt: It's really easy to use, especially with the barcode scanner - amazed that even my most obscure titles are indexed.

quotes.author: kitkatchi
quotes.text: The app has an impressive database of published cookbooks, including some rare editions. I'm so thankful that they migrated from a website to an app—the barcode scanner has made my life so much easier!
quotes.full_text:
Must-have app for cooking enthusiasts

Enormously helpful app to catalogue your cookbooks and recipes. The app has an impressive database of published cookbooks, including some rare editions. I’m so thankful that they migrated from a website to an app—the barcode scanner has made my life so much easier!
quotes.excerpt: The app has an impressive database of published cookbooks, including some rare editions. I'm so thankful that they migrated from a website to an app—the barcode scanner has made…

quotes.author: TOTCOOKS
quotes.text: So easy to add your cookbooks by scanning the bar codes and whenever you want to make something but can't remember what recipes are where you just list an ingredient you have and search all your cookbooks to find recipes.
quotes.full_text:
Game changer

So easy to add your cookbooks by scanning the bar codes and whenever you want to make something but can’t remember what recipes are where you just list an ingredient you have and search all your cookbooks to find recipes. It’s so handy; I love it!
quotes.excerpt: So easy to add your cookbooks by scanning the bar codes and whenever you want to make something but can't remember what recipes are where you just list an ingredient you have and…

quotes.author: jv4
quotes.text: I'll add more cookbooks if the Pro version pricing comes down. Right now it's too expensive to justify.
quotes.full_text:
Almost Perfect

I have the free version with my top 5 cookbooks loaded. I love being able to quickly find recipes, but even better is quickly searching by ingredient. For example, right now I have an open jar of chilies in adobo sauce to use up. I was able to instantly find quite a few recipes from my collection that use that ingredient. Perfect if you’re at home and trying to plan meals. This isn’t 5 stars for me yet because it doesn’t list quantities. So if I’m looking up a recipe while at the store or away from my cookbooks, I had to guess how much to buy. I’m sure there is licensing reasons for this, but it’s still something to be aware of. Also, I’ll add more cookbooks if the Pro version pricing comes down. Right now it’s too expensive to justify.
quotes.excerpt: I'll add more cookbooks if the Pro version pricing comes down. Right now it's too expensive to justify.

quotes.author: Cevility
quotes.text: It's obvious from even a small amount of use that this is an amazing resource, but OMG I'm struggling to accept the cost. I'm excited to use it with a first month free deal I got, but imagining keeping it long term makes me grit my teeth because of the cost.
quotes.full_text:
This price is hard to swallow…

It’s obvious from even a small amount of use that this is an amazing resource, but OMG I’m struggling to accept the cost. I’m excited to use it with a first month free deal I got, but imagining keeping it long term makes me grit my teeth because of the cost. Hope they change the way they do things. It’s a wonderful resource, but many home cooks (who often cook partly to save money) will never find out because of the ongoing $$$ drain. Say I love it and want to use it as my personal cooking library index —- in 10 years I’ll have spent $400… and that’s if the price stays the same…
quotes.excerpt: It's obvious from even a small amount of use that this is an amazing resource, but OMG I'm struggling to accept the cost. I'm excited to use it with a first month free deal I…

quotes.author: chloegrace1222
quotes.text: The app doesn't even include the recipes, the UI isn't great, and the fact that there's a monthly cost is enraging. I'd rather pay $10 or even $15 upfront if I had to as opposed to $40/year forever.
quotes.full_text:
Needs work, steep cost for what it is

I saw this advertised on social media and downloaded it immediately—what an awesome idea!! However, I was disappointed to realize you have a 5 book limit for the free version… why would I even pay monthly for the premium? For my own cookbooks? For indexing? The app doesn’t even include the recipes, the UI isn’t great, and the fact that there’s a monthly cost is enraging. I’d rather pay $10 or even $15 upfront if I had to as opposed to $40/year forever. Ended up deleting because I can’t add all my cookbooks and feels ridiculous to pay for it.
quotes.excerpt: The app doesn't even include the recipes, the UI isn't great, and the fact that there's a monthly cost is enraging. I'd rather pay $10 or even $15 upfront if I had to as opposed…

quotes.author: HS1983
quotes.text: I can't stand how everything is on a subscription model now, instead of letting you just buy it once and use it forever (I don't mind paying for updates when I'm ready to upgrade but don't want to pay monthly forever).
quotes.full_text:
Sneaky

Asks for a bunch of info then hits you with the subscription fee. I can’t stand how everything is on a subscription model now, instead of letting you just buy it once and use it forever (I don’t mind paying for updates when I’m ready to upgrade but don’t want to pay monthly forever). And to not be upfront about the cost until after getting a bunch of information out of the user doesn’t sit well with me.
quotes.excerpt: I can't stand how everything is on a subscription model now, instead of letting you just buy it once and use it forever (I don't mind paying for updates when I'm ready to upgrade…

quotes.author: welikegoodfood
quotes.text: We wish it was offered at a more affordable price point. We think it is overpriced; however now that we have it we definitely use it multiple times every week.
quotes.full_text:
We love this app

This apps unlocks the power of our 90 odd cookbooks.  We love how we can search by ingredients.  No more flipping through indexes or leafing through cookbooks with no indexes.  We have started using most of our cookbooks again.  We wish it was offered at a more affordable price point.  We think it is overpriced; however now that we have it we definitely use it multiple times every week.
quotes.excerpt: We wish it was offered at a more affordable price point. We think it is overpriced; however now that we have it we definitely use it multiple times every week.

quotes.author: You again Jane
quotes.text: I would absolutely love to pay for this app as if I were paying for an incredible cookbook: one time. I don't want to spend the time uploading all my cookbooks and getting organized digitally if there's a risk the cost of a subscription would go up each year, or with the feeling that I'm paying $40 every year for the rest of my life (easily $1600+) just to browse my own books.
quotes.full_text:
Please consider one-time payment

I would absolutely love to pay for this app as if I were paying for an incredible cookbook: one time. I don’t want to spend the time uploading all my cookbooks and getting organized digitally if there’s a risk the cost of 
a subscription would go up each year, or with the feeling that I’m paying $40 every year for the rest of my life (easily $1600+) just to browse my own books.
quotes.excerpt: I would absolutely love to pay for this app as if I were paying for an incredible cookbook: one time. I don't want to spend the time uploading all my cookbooks and getting…

quotes.author: Anchoraway1234
quotes.text: Why not be honest and upfront and list that it costs $50/ year to basically index recipe TITLES? … I can see paying a few dollars once to purchase it, but an ongoing sub? Ridiculous
quotes.full_text:
Bait and switch

Deleted it as soon as I opened it. Why not be honest and upfront and list that it costs $50/ year to basically index recipe TITLES? So let’s be clear, not the actual recipe, you still need to pull out your recipe book. And for that an annual sub costs the equivalent of a hardcover recipe book. I can see paying a few dollars once to purchase it, but an ongoing sub? Ridiculous
quotes.excerpt: Why not be honest and upfront and list that it costs $50/ year to basically index recipe TITLES? … I can see paying a few dollars once to purchase it, but an ongoing sub?…

quotes.author: AbRaPaGe
quotes.text: I'm so over subscriptions draining my bank account when they aren't necessary. I would gladly pay a lump sum for this app even if it were a bit pricey. But having a subscription that's a bit pricey is just not something I'm willing to do anymore.
quotes.full_text:
Why a subscription?

I love the idea of this app and I’m sure if I subscribed and got to add more than five books I would genuinely love it. I’m so over subscriptions draining my bank account when they aren’t necessary. I would gladly pay a lump sum for this app even if it were a bit pricey. But having a subscription that’s a bit pricey is just not something I’m willing to do anymore.
quotes.excerpt: I'm so over subscriptions draining my bank account when they aren't necessary. I would gladly pay a lump sum for this app even if it were a bit pricey. But having a subscription…

quotes.author: arugh
quotes.text: Personally wouldn't pay a $60 annual subscription for this service. Probably would pay a smaller one off price though.
quotes.full_text:
Meh

Cool concept. Search your cookbooks based on title or ingredients. But still need to get out your cookbook for quantities and method. Personally wouldn’t pay a $60 annual subscription for this service. Probably would pay a smaller one off price though.
quotes.excerpt: Personally wouldn't pay a $60 annual subscription for this service. Probably would pay a smaller one off price though.

quotes.author: AftonNegrea
quotes.text: The cost though…woof. I would pay a flat fee, but this cost ongoing, it's a bit much.
quotes.full_text:
Love it! But…

I really love this concept. And it’s a great looking app. Super intuitive. The cost though…woof. I would pay a flat fee, but this cost ongoing, it’s a bit much. Hopefully this will change in the future!
quotes.excerpt: The cost though…woof. I would pay a flat fee, but this cost ongoing, it's a bit much.

quotes.author: the-frog
quotes.text: But an ongoing $60 subscription is way too much for searching content.
quotes.full_text:
Great idea, massively overpriced

It’s a cool idea, I would use my books more and would absolutely pay a small convenience fee. But an ongoing $60 subscription is way too much for searching content.
quotes.excerpt: But an ongoing $60 subscription is way too much for searching content.

quotes.author: Dude1726?
quotes.text: The cost is way to high, I already own the books so why would I pay a monthly cost to get access to books I already have.
quotes.full_text:
Seems like a great idea but

The cost is way to high, I already own the books so why would I pay a monthly cost to get access to books I already have. A one time fee sure but a subscription na
quotes.excerpt: The cost is way to high, I already own the books so why would I pay a monthly cost to get access to books I already have.

quotes.author: Biancamay13
quotes.text: But at 40 a year it's just too much for me.
quotes.full_text:
Really cool idea

Love the concept. But at 40 a year it’s just too much for me. Was a neat app and it was really easy to use!
quotes.excerpt: But at 40 a year it's just too much for me.

quotes.author: CG233
quotes.text: Now, the only reason for the 4 star rate is because the app had missing ingredients on the list.
quotes.full_text:
Traveling Kitchen

I’m liking this app so far. Especially when I’m at the grocery store & i want to meal plan/meal prep for the night or the week. It is a little disappointing that the ingredients list don’t give to the measurements from the book. But no big deal. It is also handy that it gives you the page number for the recipe so you can go home & pop open the book when ready to start cooking. Now, the only reason for the 4 star rate is because the app had missing ingredients on the list. I made a peanut butter cake from an old Betty Crocker cookbook that my grandma passed down to me, & it was missing items on list. Thankfully it was a recipe I made before discovering this app. Otherwise, a trip back to the store would have been a pain.
quotes.excerpt: Now, the only reason for the 4 star rate is because the app had missing ingredients on the list.

quotes.author: G3rmany
quotes.text: Sadly a lot of my books weren't on the app at all, and many of the ones that were had not been indexed (which entirely defeats the point of the app, isn't the point to pay for the index???).
quotes.full_text:
Great idea but library lacking

Downloaded this app after seeing it advertised, what a great idea! Sadly a lot of my books weren’t on the app at all, and many of the ones that were had not been indexed (which entirely defeats the point of the app, isn’t the point to pay for the index???). After 5 scans it prompted me to subscribe, but having had almost half my collection missing from the app there’s no incentive for me to subscribe. For any value for money the library needs to be extended much wider - many of my books are very popular books. For now I’ve loaded the 5 books I rarely look at and will see how I get on with those
quotes.excerpt: Sadly a lot of my books weren't on the app at all, and many of the ones that were had not been indexed (which entirely defeats the point of the app, isn't the point to pay for…

quotes.author: Jamison2017!
quotes.text: A little misleading.
quotes.full_text:
Just ok

The app does in fact log your books and tracks the recipes. It however does not allow you to take the recipe with you and give you the step by step or quantities. A little misleading. It will give you an ingredient list but that does not always guarantee they are correct. I’ve found a few in the short time I used the free trial that were incorrect. If you added the full recipes to the page it would be worth much more. But this stage is definitely not worth paying for.
quotes.excerpt: A little misleading.

quotes.author: Dhjbxxbjea
quotes.text: I tried this and most of my recipe books weren't in the index.
quotes.full_text:
Limited use

I tried this and most of my recipe books weren’t in the index. There’s also no category browsing, like any standard table of contents, it’s just search by ingredient.

Also, the app only contains the ingredients, it doesn’t include the method; now, there may well be good copyright reasons for that, but the point is if you think this app will replace your recipe books in the kitchen, it won’t. It’s just a search engine for some of your books.
quotes.excerpt: I tried this and most of my recipe books weren't in the index.

quotes.author: 5arah76
quotes.text: Only about half my recipe books could be found.
quotes.full_text:
Great Idea But…

I really like this concept but unfortunately it doesn’t deliver on search functionality. Only about half my recipe books could be found. Of those, that it did find, I tested searching for recipes I know are in my books, but it did not return any results even when being very specific or more vague on ingredients. Too bad.
quotes.excerpt: Only about half my recipe books could be found.

quotes.author: vegaschef
quotes.text: I tried 10 of my cookbooks and only 1 was found.
quotes.full_text:
Great idea, but…

Great idea and concept. I downloaded the app and tried scanning several of my cookbooks, you’re limited to 5 until you subscribe.  Anyhow I tried 10 of my cookbooks and only 1 was found. Uninstalled…..maybe down the road I’ll try again……but I doubt it.
quotes.excerpt: I tried 10 of my cookbooks and only 1 was found.

quotes.author: Cookie Crumbs
quotes.text: And when I got done scanning more than 100 cookbooks, I found out that many of them aren't indexed or only have one or two recipes indexed.
quotes.full_text:
Great in theory, not in Application

I’ve been waiting for an app like this for a very long time. And when I got done scanning more than 100 cookbooks, I found out that many of them aren’t indexed or only have one or two recipes indexed. So, a waste of an afternoon and a waste of money.
quotes.excerpt: And when I got done scanning more than 100 cookbooks, I found out that many of them aren't indexed or only have one or two recipes indexed.

quotes.author: MRS.AES123
quotes.text: I love the idea but most of my cookbooks are not in their data base so you can request to have it indexed or index it yourself.
quotes.full_text:
Doesn’t have a big selection

I love the idea but most of my cookbooks are not in their data base so you can request to have it indexed or index it yourself . But first you need to read the indexing module. A lot of work but worth it in the end.
quotes.excerpt: I love the idea but most of my cookbooks are not in their data base so you can request to have it indexed or index it yourself.

quotes.author: MidwestBeads
quotes.text: I was so excited to start scanning my cookbooks but the ones I scanned were not indexed yet. Making the app a bit pointless.
quotes.full_text:
Cookbooks Not Indexed

I was so excited to start scanning my cookbooks but the ones I scanned were not indexed yet. Making the app a bit pointless. 
If I am paying for the app I was hoping that my cookbooks would be ready to go.

quotes.author: J.L.C.B.
quotes.text: Then brokenhearted to realize that it doesn't have the recipe to read & follow, just directs you to that recipe. You still gotta pull out the book, mess with propping it open.
quotes.full_text:
Brilliant but misses it

I was knocked out by how easy it was to scan and see my cookbook library appear on the app. Then brokenhearted to realize that it doesn’t have the recipe to read & follow, just directs you to that recipe. You still gotta pull out the book, mess with propping it open. I kind of love reading & doing recipes off my tablet, so this is a dealbreaker for me😢
quotes.excerpt: Then brokenhearted to realize that it doesn't have the recipe to read & follow, just directs you to that recipe. You still gotta pull out the book, mess with propping it open.

quotes.author: Edit Faris
quotes.text: There are no measurements/ingredient amounts showing in the recipes and it admittedly could leave out ingredients. The recipes direct you back to the cookbook.
quotes.full_text:
Very disappointed

I was so looking forward to upload my favorite books and use the recipes from my phone or tablet. There are no measurements/ingredient amounts showing in the recipes and it admittedly could leave out ingredients. The recipes direct you back to the cookbook. With upgrades, this is a great idea but without the full recipes, it’s not practical at all.
quotes.excerpt: There are no measurements/ingredient amounts showing in the recipes and it admittedly could leave out ingredients. The recipes direct you back to the cookbook.

quotes.author: arugh
quotes.text: Search your cookbooks based on title or ingredients. But still need to get out your cookbook for quantities and method.
quotes.full_text:
Meh

Cool concept. Search your cookbooks based on title or ingredients. But still need to get out your cookbook for quantities and method. Personally wouldn’t pay a $60 annual subscription for this service. Probably would pay a smaller one off price though.
quotes.excerpt: Search your cookbooks based on title or ingredients. But still need to get out your cookbook for quantities and method.

quotes.author: bad5026
quotes.text: I scanned 3 cookbooks, and I don't see any recipes, only an ingredients list.
quotes.full_text:
no actual recipes in app?

I am so confused by this app. I scanned 3 cookbooks, and I don’t see any recipes, only an ingredients list. Makes it quite pointless, no? I’ll be canceling before my free trial is up.
quotes.excerpt: I scanned 3 cookbooks, and I don't see any recipes, only an ingredients list.

quotes.author: Mhysas Dragon
quotes.text: It scanned the ISBN's of my cookbooks but most needed to be indexed into the app and those that were there, tapped on a recipe and it would show me the ingredients but no directions
quotes.full_text:
Not as advertised

It scanned the ISBN’s of my cookbooks but most needed to be indexed into the app and those that were there, tapped on a recipe and it would show me the ingredients but no directions
quotes.excerpt: It scanned the ISBN's of my cookbooks but most needed to be indexed into the app and those that were there, tapped on a recipe and it would show me the ingredients but no…

quotes.author: Peteralt2
quotes.text: The ingredients are listed but not recipes.
quotes.full_text:
No recipes

The ingredients are listed but not recipes. I guess this is a copyright issue that publishers blocked. Am I right?
quotes.excerpt: The ingredients are listed but not recipes.

quotes.author: desperate to delete
quotes.text: Nowhere does it mention that this app requires payment to use – you only find out after you've signed up and created an account.
quotes.full_text:
Be careful!

⚠️ MISLEADING – READ BEFORE DOWNLOADING
Do not be fooled by the description. Nowhere does it mention that this app requires payment to use – you only find out after you’ve signed up and created an account. By that point, you’re already locked in, and to make matters worse, there is no way to delete your account.
This is completely unacceptable. The pricing should be made clear upfront, before anyone hands over their personal details. Consider yourself warned – avoid this app until they are transparent about their costs and give users the ability to remove their data.
quotes.excerpt: Nowhere does it mention that this app requires payment to use – you only find out after you've signed up and created an account.

quotes.author: HS1983
quotes.text: Asks for a bunch of info then hits you with the subscription fee. And to not be upfront about the cost until after getting a bunch of information out of the user doesn't sit well with me.
quotes.full_text:
Sneaky

Asks for a bunch of info then hits you with the subscription fee. I can’t stand how everything is on a subscription model now, instead of letting you just buy it once and use it forever (I don’t mind paying for updates when I’m ready to upgrade but don’t want to pay monthly forever). And to not be upfront about the cost until after getting a bunch of information out of the user doesn’t sit well with me.
quotes.excerpt: Asks for a bunch of info then hits you with the subscription fee. And to not be upfront about the cost until after getting a bunch of information out of the user doesn't sit well…

quotes.author: Anchoraway1234
quotes.text: Why not be honest and upfront and list that it costs $50/ year to basically index recipe TITLES?
quotes.full_text:
Bait and switch

Deleted it as soon as I opened it. Why not be honest and upfront and list that it costs $50/ year to basically index recipe TITLES? So let’s be clear, not the actual recipe, you still need to pull out your recipe book. And for that an annual sub costs the equivalent of a hardcover recipe book. I can see paying a few dollars once to purchase it, but an ongoing sub? Ridiculous
quotes.excerpt: Why not be honest and upfront and list that it costs $50/ year to basically index recipe TITLES?

quotes.author: Bianka J
quotes.text: Nowhere does it mention that you have to pay for this app until it asks you to create an account.
quotes.full_text:
Misleading!! You have to pay for it

Nowhere does it mention that you have to pay for this app until it asks you to create an account.
Deleted my account immediately.
quotes.excerpt: Nowhere does it mention that you have to pay for this app until it asks you to create an account.

takeaways.title: Showcase ingredient search in first-use moments
takeaways.body: Twenty-two reviewers led with ingredient search as their main win—make it the hero feature early. Users describe it as a time-saver: 'quickly search by ingredient' and 'quickly find recipes' appear in multiple reviews. One reviewer contrasted years of manual flipping through cookbooks against instant ingredient lookup, showing the pain you solved. Highlight this or initial product tour so new users see value before they hit paywalls.
takeaways.summary: Twenty-two reviewers led with ingredient search as their main win—make it the hero feature early.
points: Users describe it as a time-saver: 'quickly search by ingredient' and 'quickly find recipes' appear in multiple reviews.
One reviewer contrasted years of manual flipping through cookbooks against instant ingredient lookup, showing the pain you solved.
Highlight this or initial product tour so new users see value before they hit paywalls.

takeaways.title: Highlight rediscovery as the emotional payoff
takeaways.body: Fourteen reviewers specifically mentioned rekindling their relationship with forgotten cookbooks—lean into that story. One reviewer called it 'restoration of usefulness' and 'rediscoveries await,' framing the app as a second life for old books. Another said the app 'helped me rediscover lost loves and find new ones'—emotional language that sells better than 'indexing.' This angle appeals to cookbook collectors and ties directly to guilt about unread shelves.
takeaways.summary: Fourteen reviewers specifically mentioned rekindling their relationship with forgotten cookbooks—lean into that story.
points: One reviewer called it 'restoration of usefulness' and 'rediscoveries await,' framing the app as a second life for old books.
Another said the app 'helped me rediscover lost loves and find new ones'—emotional language that sells better than 'indexing.'
This angle appeals to cookbook collectors and ties directly to guilt about unread shelves.

takeaways.title: Own the niche: built for massive cookbook libraries
takeaways.body: You solve a real problem for people with thousands of dollars in cookbooks—don't hide behind vague positioning. One reviewer called it 'a unique niche: it's for the cook who owns too many cookbooks'—that's permission to own that audience. Multiple reviewers mentioned multi-thousand-dollar collections gathering dust, and you're the tool that unlocks them. Stop trying to be for everyone; make it obvious you're built for serious cookbook collectors.
takeaways.summary: You solve a real problem for people with thousands of dollars in cookbooks—don't hide behind vague positioning.
points: One reviewer called it 'a unique niche: it's for the cook who owns too many cookbooks'—that's permission to own that audience.
Multiple reviewers mentioned multi-thousand-dollar collections gathering dust, and you're the tool that unlocks them.
Stop trying to be for everyone; make it obvious you're built for serious cookbook collectors.

takeaways.title: Feature barcode scanning prominently
takeaways.body: Barcode scanning delights users and removes friction—three reviewers unprompted praised it for ease and database depth. One reviewer was 'amazed that even my most obscure titles are indexed,' suggesting your database and scanner accuracy are underrated. Another noted the scanner made migration from web to app worthwhile on its own. This is a concrete, tangible feature you can demo—use it to justify the app over online alternatives.
takeaways.summary: Barcode scanning delights users and removes friction—three reviewers unprompted praised it for ease and database depth.
points: One reviewer was 'amazed that even my most obscure titles are indexed,' suggesting your database and scanner accuracy are underrated.
Another noted the scanner made migration from web to app worthwhile on its own.
This is a concrete, tangible feature you can demo—use it to justify the app over online alternatives.

takeaways.title: Show pricing before asking for signup details
takeaways.body: Four reviewers felt blindsided by paywalls after entering personal info—disclose cost upfront to stop friction and anger. One user wrote: 'Nowhere does it mention that this app requires payment to use – you only find out after you've signed up.' Another criticized the order: 'Asks for a bunch of info then hits you with the subscription fee,' calling it dishonest. Move the price to the top of the funnel so people can decide before investing time or data.
takeaways.summary: Four reviewers felt blindsided by paywalls after entering personal info—disclose cost upfront to stop friction and anger.
points: One user wrote: 'Nowhere does it mention that this app requires payment to use – you only find out after you've signed up.'
Another criticized the order: 'Asks for a bunch of info then hits you with the subscription fee,' calling it dishonest.
Move the price to the top of the funnel so people can decide before investing time or data.

takeaways.title: Offer a one-time purchase tier
takeaways.body: Nine reviewers explicitly rejected the subscription model and asked for a one-time buy—this is real demand, not noise. One reviewer said: 'I'd rather pay $10 or even $15 upfront if I had to as opposed to $40/year forever.' Another stated: 'I would absolutely love to pay for this app as if I were paying for an incredible cookbook: one time.' A third expressed subscription fatigue: 'I can't stand how everything is on a subscription model now.'—consider a perpetual license option.
takeaways.summary: Nine reviewers explicitly rejected the subscription model and asked for a one-time buy—this is real demand, not noise.
points: One reviewer said: 'I'd rather pay $10 or even $15 upfront if I had to as opposed to $40/year forever.'
Another stated: 'I would absolutely love to pay for this app as if I were paying for an incredible cookbook: one time.'
A third expressed subscription fatigue: 'I can't stand how everything is on a subscription model now.'—consider a perpetual license option.

takeaways.title: Lower subscription price or justify current cost
takeaways.body: Thirteen reviewers cite price as a barrier to upgrade—either drop it or explain what makes Pro worth the cost. One said: 'I'll add more cookbooks if the Pro version pricing comes down. Right now it's too expensive to justify.' Another: 'I'm struggling to accept the cost,' even while acknowledging it's 'an amazing resource.' The tension is real—people love the app but won't pay. Test lower tiers or bundle with annual discounts.
takeaways.summary: Thirteen reviewers cite price as a barrier to upgrade—either drop it or explain what makes Pro worth the cost.
points: One said: 'I'll add more cookbooks if the Pro version pricing comes down. Right now it's too expensive to justify.'
Another: 'I'm struggling to accept the cost,' even while acknowledging it's 'an amazing resource.'
The tension is real—people love the app but won't pay. Test lower tiers or bundle with annual discounts.

takeaways.title: Index more cookbooks and complete existing ones
takeaways.body: Eleven reviewers found missing cookbooks or incomplete indexing, breaking the core promise—audit and backfill your database. One user: 'a lot of my books weren't on the app at all, and many of the ones that were had not been indexed.' Another: 'the app had missing ingredients on the list,' enough to dock a star. Incomplete indexing 'entirely defeats the point of the app'—prioritize data quality over feature adds.
takeaways.summary: Eleven reviewers found missing cookbooks or incomplete indexing, breaking the core promise—audit and backfill your database.
points: One user: 'a lot of my books weren't on the app at all, and many of the ones that were had not been indexed.'
Another: 'the app had missing ingredients on the list,' enough to dock a star.
Incomplete indexing 'entirely defeats the point of the app'—prioritize data quality over feature adds.

takeaways.title: Let users view full recipes in-app for premium tier
takeaways.body: Eight reviewers want recipe text inside the app for cooking at the stove—OCR or license recipe text from publishers to unlock a real paid differentiator. One user: 'doesn't have the recipe to read & follow, just directs you to that recipe. You still gotta pull out the book.' Another felt 'brokenhearted to realize that it doesn't have the recipe'—that's unmet demand at premium price point. Showing recipe text would justify a subscription and eliminate the 'just an index' complaint that fuels pricing anger.
takeaways.summary: Eight reviewers want recipe text inside the app for cooking at the stove—OCR or license recipe text from publishers to unlock a real paid differentiator.
points: One user: 'doesn't have the recipe to read & follow, just directs you to that recipe. You still gotta pull out the book.'
Another felt 'brokenhearted to realize that it doesn't have the recipe'—that's unmet demand at premium price point.
Showing recipe text would justify a subscription and eliminate the 'just an index' complaint that fuels pricing anger.

takeaways.title: Bundle meal planning with grocery-store search
takeaways.body: Six reviewers mention using the app at the grocery store to plan meals—turn that moment into a whole workflow. Users said: 'when I'm at the grocery store & i want to meal plan/meal prep,' the app helps them decide what to cook on the fly. One noted: 'you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store'—this is a use case, not a feature yet. Build shopping lists, meal prep calendars, or pantry tracking to make the grocery-store moment sticky and defensible.
takeaways.summary: Six reviewers mention using the app at the grocery store to plan meals—turn that moment into a whole workflow.
points: Users said: 'when I'm at the grocery store & i want to meal plan/meal prep,' the app helps them decide what to cook on the fly.
One noted: 'you can check the ingredients of a recipe while you are at the grocery store'—this is a use case, not a feature yet.
Build shopping lists, meal prep calendars, or pantry tracking to make the grocery-store moment sticky and defensible.

takeaways.title: Create a 'revival' onboarding flow for dusty shelves
takeaways.body: Fourteen reviewers celebrate rediscovery—build a dedicated experience that helps new users catalog forgotten books and find first wins fast. Users feel relief: 'restore the usefulness of those cookbooks that you bought over the years. Rediscoveries await.' Market this as the 'unlock your shelf' story and lead new users through barcode scanning to their first 'aha' ingredient search. This frames the subscription as investment in a collection they already own, softening the price objection.
takeaways.summary: Fourteen reviewers celebrate rediscovery—build a dedicated experience that helps new users catalog forgotten books and find first wins fast.
points: Users feel relief: 'restore the usefulness of those cookbooks that you bought over the years. Rediscoveries await.'
Market this as the 'unlock your shelf' story and lead new users through barcode scanning to their first 'aha' ingredient search.
This frames the subscription as investment in a collection they already own, softening the price objection.

takeaways.title: Partner with cookbook publishers to close indexing gaps
takeaways.body: Eleven reviewers hit dead-end searches or incomplete data—pitch publishers on licensing rights to OCR and index their full backlists. One reviewer said: 'a lot of my books weren't on the app at all'—but also praised the 'impressive database of published cookbooks.' You've proven the concept; now scale it by working with publishers directly instead of user submissions or freelance indexing. Complete indexing becomes a competitive moat and justifies the cost to skeptics who currently see an 'unfinished product.'
takeaways.summary: Eleven reviewers hit dead-end searches or incomplete data—pitch publishers on licensing rights to OCR and index their full backlists.
points: One reviewer said: 'a lot of my books weren't on the app at all'—but also praised the 'impressive database of published cookbooks.'
You've proven the concept; now scale it by working with publishers directly instead of user submissions or freelance indexing.
Complete indexing becomes a competitive moat and justifies the cost to skeptics who currently see an 'unfinished product.'
